meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 8)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Advanced practice: choose singular or plural based on number clues.

questions.question: What is the plural of "shell"?
questions.explanation: shell → shells. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: "Bottles" is the plural of "bottle".
questions.explanation: Yes! bottle → bottles. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Write the plural of "bear".
questions.explanation: bear → bears. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: She finds ten ___ on the beach. (shell)
questions.explanation: "ten" tells us we need a plural. shell → shells.

questions.question: The plural of "class" is "classs".
questions.explanation: class ends in -ss, so the plural is classes (add -es), not classs.

questions.question: Write the plural of "bottle".
questions.explanation: bottle → bottles. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Which sentence uses the correct plural?
questions.explanation: apple → apples. "seven" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: Sort these words: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: shell/bear/class = one (singular). bottles/shells = more than one (plural).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Three bears sat by the tree."

questions.question: The plural of "dish" is "dishes".
questions.explanation: Yes! dish ends in -sh, so dish → dishes (add -es).

questions.question: Write the plural of "class".
questions.explanation: class → classes. Words ending in -ss add -es.

questions.question: Which noun uses -es because it ends in -ss?
questions.explanation: class ends in -ss, so class → classes (add -es).

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -sh or -ss add -es.

questions.question: In the sentence "One bear sat by the tree," the noun "bear" is singular.
questions.explanation: "One bear" means just one bear — that's singular.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Roy drinks a glass of juice."

questions.question: Write the plural of "pencil".
questions.explanation: pencil → pencils. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I open my book to page 8.
I open my bookes to page 8.
I open my books to page 8.
I open my bookies to page 8.
questions.explanation: "my book" (singular, one book) is correct here — no number clue for plural.

questions.question: Which rule explains why 'fox → foxes'?
questions.explanation: Words ending in -x (like fox) add -es to form the plural: foxes.

questions.question: In the sentence "There are many star in the sky," the sentence is correct.
questions.explanation: "many" tells us we need plural: stars (not star). Correct: "There are many stars in the sky."

questions.question: Write the plural of "road".
questions.explanation: road → roads. Regular nouns add -s.
